🕰️ 21. History Timeline — First Analysis to Now
Last refreshed: Jul 4, 2026

Chronological record of every analysis, correction, and structural change to this document. Newest at the bottom, since this is a timeline (unlike Section 19's Weekly Update Log, which stays newest-first).

Jun 13, 2026 — Original Analysis

Agency/analyst-prepared homepage CRO roadmap. Established the 32-test roadmap (Weeks 1–4), baseline metrics (CR 0.08%, ATC 1.24%, checkout abandonment 86.3%), and competitor benchmark table. Baseline traffic assumption used throughout: ~600K sessions/month — later found to be significantly understated (see Jul 4 entry below).

Jun 22, 2026 — Live Results Update

Category grid rollout (Jun 11) validated as not causing the Living Room Sets / XL Sofas share drop — traced instead to a Meta/Instagram campaign pause and Google Shopping feed issue predating the grid change. Klaviyo WhatsApp channel identified as outperforming email 6.3:1.

Jul 2, 2026 — Initial GSC/SEO Cross-Check

First conversation thread: audited an agency-prepared GSC performance report against a raw GSC export. Found a homepage average-position discrepancy (3.7 claimed vs 11.8 raw) and an 8-month gap in the underlying Chart.csv data. This became the template for the accuracy-checking approach applied to every subsequent data pull in this project.

Jul 4, 2026 (morning) — 30-Day Sprint Plan, PLP Track, SEO Tab, Weekly Log Structure

Added: the 30-Day A/B Test Sprint Plan (Section 16), PLP Track (Section 17), corrections to Test 30 and Test 22 revenue math (Section 18), a lightweight SEO Catalog Audit tab (Section 20) linked to a separate SEO Dashboard React file, and the Weekly Update Log structure (Section 19). Tab navigation converted from single-page scroll to a grouped 6-column grid.

Jul 4, 2026 (afternoon) — New Data Pull: GA4, GSC, PageSpeed

New Jan 1–Jul 4, 2026 exports reviewed: GA4 (Engagement, Traffic Acquisition, Events), GSC (Coverage ×3 scopes, Performance-on-Search, Core Web Vitals ×2 devices, Merchant Listings, Product Snippets), and live PageSpeed Insights (mobile + desktop). Found: the new GSC export's Chart.csv has a genuine ~4-month gap (Jan 1–Apr 27 missing despite the stated Jan 1–Jul 4 filter); two GA4 CSV exports were capped at the 100,000-row limit with a resulting ~2.6% discrepancy between them; and total GA4 sessions for the period (14,880,323) appeared ~4x higher than the original roadmap's ~600K/month baseline — flagged for verification rather than resolved at this point.

Jul 4, 2026 (later) — Critical Discovery: Wrong Store Connected

Attempted to resolve the session discrepancy via live Shopify data. Initial connection returned only 13 total orders for the Jan 1–Jul 4 window — later found to be because the connected store was Roots Furniture (a vendor/sister brand), not alrugaibfurniture.com. Switched to the correct store. Access scope check confirmed read_all_orders, read_analytics, and read_reports were granted — broader access than initially assumed.

Jul 4, 2026 (later) — Session/CR Baseline Corrected

With the correct store connected, ShopifyQL queries returned: 12,393,940 total sessions (Jan 1–Jul 4, 2026) — confirming, not contradicting, GA4's 14,880,323 figure (within ~17%, a normal cross-tool variance). This corrects the roadmap's foundational traffic assumption from ~600K/month to ~2.0–2.5M/month. Also found: Shopify's order-based CR (0.143%) is roughly double GA4's purchase-event CR (0.077%) for the identical window — plausibly explained by offsite payment-redirect tracking loss (unconfirmed). Orders total (17,703) and AOV (SAR 2,530) both cross-checked internally consistent across two independent ShopifyQL queries.

Jul 4, 2026 (later) — Ashley Homestore Side Analysis

Pulled the same metrics for Ashley Homestore Furniture (store.ashley.sa) — a fully separate Shopify store, distinct from the "Ashley Homestore" vendor-tagged products inside Al Rugaib's own catalog. Ashley: 4,945,945 sessions, 2,543 orders, SAR 5,561,399 sales, CR 0.051%, Jan 1–Jul 4, 2026. Flagged an unexplained April session spike (35% of the 6-month total in one month) as needing verification before use in any trend analysis. Held explicitly as reference data only — no CRO recommendations built on it per user instruction.

Jul 4, 2026 (current) — Sprint Retargeted to Corrected Baseline + Benchmark Research

This update: corrected the Executive Summary's baseline framing (Section 1), added this History Timeline (Section 21), and retargeted the 30-Day Sprint Plan's metrics and revenue-impact math to the corrected ~2.0–2.5M sessions/month baseline (Section 16). Searched for KSA/furniture-specific CRO benchmarks — found global furniture e-commerce CVR benchmarks (0.8–1.6%, multiple sources) and Saudi market-wide (all-category, not furniture-specific) ATC/cart-abandonment figures (ECDB, 2025), but no verified KSA-specific furniture-industry benchmark exists in what was found. Targets in Section 16 are set against the closest verifiable references available, with that gap stated explicitly rather than filled with an invented number.

Al Rugaib Furniture — Homepage CRO Roadmap & 30-Day Sprint Plan

Original analysis Jun 13, 2026 · Live results updated Jun 22, 2026 · 30-Day Sprint Plan and data corrections added Jul 4, 2026 · Weekly Update Log structure added Jul 4, 2026 · Session/CR baseline corrected and History Timeline added Jul 4, 2026

GA4 export Mar 15–Jun 12, 2026 · Grid rolled out Jun 11, 2026 · Shopify data Jun 8–22, 2026 · Klaviyo data Jun 2026 · GA4/Shopify Jan 1–Jul 4, 2026 cross-check confirms ~2.0–2.5M sessions/month (supersedes earlier ~600K/month baseline)

32 tests + 30-day execution sprint + PLP track + weekly log + history timeline · Tests 31–32 activate after GA4 CTR events · WhatsApp = primary revenue channel · Email CTA fix required before next send · See Section 18 for correction log, Section 19 for weekly history, Section 21 for full timeline
